briefs.title: Council to vote on North Road BIA renewal and e-bike bylaw changes
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on several items at its April 14, 2026 meeting, including whether to renew the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where local businesses collectively fund area improvements — and proposed bylaw changes that would allow e-bike share and electric kick scooter programs on city streets and in parks.
briefs.action_required: If you operate on North Road, check the BIA renewal outcome — it affects your mandatory BIA levy.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — North Road BIA renewal and e-bike bylaw vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on North Road BIA renewal and e-bike bylaw changes in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on several items at its April 14, 2026 meeting, including whether to renew the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where local businesses collectively fund area improvements — for another five years. Council will also consider proposed changes to city bylaws that would allow an e-bike share pilot program and address provincial rules for electric kick scooters on Burnaby streets and in parks.
briefs.action_required: If you operate on North Road, check the BIA renewal outcome — it affects your business levy.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — North Road BIA renewal and e-bike bylaw vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby March 24, 2026 Meeting — Key Decisions on Housing, Zoning & Fees
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on several items affecting local businesses and property owners, including rezoning applications for mixed-use and self-storage developments, final adoption of updated parking meter and fees bylaws, and a $4.2M water metering contract. Notably, the minutes from this meeting are now up for approval at the April 14, 2026 Council meeting — meaning these decisions are being formally confirmed, not yet fully settled.
briefs.action_required: Review the adopted fee and parking meter bylaw changes — they may affect your operating costs in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — approval of March 24 minutes scheduled

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby March 24, 2026 Meeting Minutes Up for Adoption
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on adopting the draft minutes from its March 24, 2026 meeting at the upcoming April 14, 2026 meeting. That March session covered several decisions relevant to business owners, including a $4.2M water metering contract, rezoning proposals for mixed-use and self-storage developments, updates to parking meter and fees bylaws, and a new Poverty Reduction Strategy.
briefs.action_required: Review the March 24 decisions — especially rezoning and bylaw changes — to see if your business or property is affected.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Council to Consider Letter Urging Federal Action on Chlorine Rail Transport Safety
briefs.summary: At a Special Council meeting on April 1, 2026, Burnaby Council voted unanimously to have Mayor Hurley write a letter to Canada's Minister of Transport raising serious safety concerns about liquid chlorine being transported by rail through densely populated areas of the Lower Mainland. The concern centres on a chlorine plant in North Vancouver that ships an average of four rail cars of liquid chlorine through Burnaby every day — a risk that presenters say has not been fully assessed in a neighbouring municipality's proposed bylaw change.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near Burnaby's rail corridor, monitor federal and municipal responses to this chlorine transport safety issue.

key_dates.label: District of North Vancouver chlorine bylaw amendment — third reading scheduled

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Day of Mourning Proclamation for Workers Killed or Injured on the Job
briefs.summary: Council will consider a request from the New Westminster & District Labour Council to officially declare April 28th as the 'Day of Mourning for Workers Killed and Injured on the Job.' The group is also asking Council to observe a minute of silence at the April 14, 2026 meeting and to encourage city staff to do the same at 11:00 a.m. on April 28th.
briefs.action_required: If worker safety matters to your business, attend the April 14 Council meeting or watch the livestream.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Day of Mourning delegation presentation
Day of Mourning for Workers Killed and Injured on the Job — April 28

briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Construction & Development, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Declaring April 28th Day of Mourning for Workers
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to hear a delegation from the New Westminster & District Labour Council requesting that the City officially declare April 28th as the 'Day of Mourning for Workers Killed and Injured on the Job.' The group also asks Council to observe a minute of silence at the April 14 meeting and encourage city staff to do the same at 11:00 a.m. on April 28th.
briefs.action_required: If worker safety matters to your business, consider attending the April 14 Council meeting to hear the presentation.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council delegation — Day of Mourning presentation
Day of Mourning for Workers — minute of silence observed

briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Construction & Development, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on North Road BIA renewal, e-bike rules, and housing updates
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on several items at its April 14, 2026 meeting. Key items include whether to proceed with renewing the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where local businesses collectively fund area improvements — plus proposed bylaw changes that would set rules for e-bike share and electric kick scooter programs on Burnaby streets and in parks.
briefs.action_required: If your business is in the North Road BIA, check the petition results — enough objections were received to affect the renewal vote.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — North Road BIA renewal and e-bike bylaw vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on appointing new Chief Election Officer for Burnaby's 2026 election
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on appointing Dennis Back as the new Chief Election Officer for the October 17, 2026 General Local Election. The previous officer, Samantha Pellizzari, is on leave and is not expected to return before election day, so a replacement is needed to keep election planning on track.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this affects who oversees the October 2026 Burnaby election.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026 General Local Election day

briefs.title: Council to Consider: North Road BIA Renewal Blocked by Sufficient Owner Opposition
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report showing that enough property owners opposed the renewal of the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where businesses pay an extra tax to fund local improvements — to block it from moving forward. More than 50% of property owners by both parcel count (55%) and assessed value (52%) signed petitions against the renewal, meeting the legal threshold that prevents Council from adopting the renewal bylaw.
briefs.action_required: If you are a North Road BIA member, contact the Association now — renewal has failed and grant funding will stop.
briefs.industries: Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider: North Road BIA Renewal Blocked by Property Owner Opposition
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report showing that enough property owners on North Road voted against renewing the Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where businesses pay an extra tax to fund local improvements — to stop the renewal from moving forward. Over 55% of affected property owners (representing 52% of total property value) submitted formal opposition, which meets the legal threshold to block the bylaw. Council will consider accepting this result and notifying the North Road Business Improvement Association that the renewal has failed.
briefs.action_required: If you own or rent on North Road, watch for updates — the BIA renewal has failed and no BIA tax will be collected in 2026.
briefs.industries: Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby Housing Authority annual governance and board appointments for 2026
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on routine annual housekeeping matters for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) — a city-owned housing company. If approved, Council would skip a formal annual general meeting (AGM), appoint KPMG as auditor, confirm the current board of directors for two- or three-year terms, and set December 12, 2026 as the official annual reference date (the date the AGM would otherwise have been held).
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if you work with or follow the Burnaby Housing Authority.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Housing Authority annual reference date — BHA corporate governance

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Housing Authority 2025 Financials and 2026 Budget
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) first-ever audited financial statements and its 2026 operating budget at the April 14, 2026 meeting. Accepting these documents would unlock $2 million in City operating funding for the BHA in 2026, supporting two major rental housing projects — one at 10th Avenue & Newcombe Street (204 units) and one at Marine Drive in Edmonds (183 townhomes).
briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's decision — it unlocks $2M for new rental housing that may affect local rental supply.

key_dates.label: BHA $25M investment certificate matures — November 2026

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to vote on bylaws enabling e-bike share and e-scooter programs in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on amendments to three city bylaws that would officially allow a new shared e-bike rental program and set rules for privately owned electric kick scooters on city streets and in parks. The proposed changes would define where these devices can be ridden and parked, give the city authority to create designated parking zones, and set out fines for riding in areas not approved for these devices.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — new riding and parking rules could affect your business or deliveries.

key_dates.label: Burnaby e-bike share and e-scooter bylaw amendments — council vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on bylaw changes for e-bike share and e-scooter programs in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on amendments to three city bylaws that would set the rules for a new shared e-bike rental program and bring city rules in line with BC's electric kick scooter pilot program (which lets people ride privately owned e-scooters on Burnaby streets). The proposed changes would define where these devices can be ridden and parked, create designated parking zones for shared e-bikes, and set fines of $200 for riding in areas not approved for these devices.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — new riding and parking rules for e-bikes and e-scooters could affect your business operations.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — e-bike share and e-scooter bylaw amendments vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby's First 6-Month Housing Target Progress Report
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on receiving Burnaby's first progress report under the Province's Housing Target Order — a provincial rule requiring Burnaby to build 10,240 net new homes by 2030. The report shows 910 new units were completed in the first six months, putting the city on track so far, but warns that slowing construction and a pause in provincial affordable housing funding could make future targets harder to meet.
briefs.action_required: If you develop, build, or rent property in Burnaby, monitor this report — housing targets will shape zoning and approval rules.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Housing Target Order Year 1b report deadline — BC Housing Supply Act
Burnaby Development Permit system (Form & Character, Streamside, Tenant Protection) takes effect
Burnaby Housing Target Order Year 2 reporting period begins — BC Housing Supply Act
Burnaby Housing Target Order 5-year compliance deadline — BC Housing Supply Act

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on continuing Burnaby's Building Benchmark BC energy tracking program
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on whether to authorize Burnaby's continued participation in Building Benchmark BC (Year 7), a voluntary program that helps building owners track and compare their energy use and carbon emissions. If approved, the City would contribute roughly $16,500 — already budgeted — to keep 30 civic buildings enrolled and encourage more private building owners in Burnaby to join for free.
briefs.action_required: If you own a commercial or multi-unit building in Burnaby, watch for the City's Year 7 recruitment campaign to join the free program.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Building Benchmark BC Year 7 participation approval

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Rejoining Building Energy Benchmarking Program for Year 7
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on authorizing staff to continue the city's participation in Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) for Year 7 (2026). If approved, the city would spend approximately $16,500 from its existing Planning and Development budget to stay in this voluntary program, which helps building owners track and compare their energy use and carbon emissions — at no extra cost to those owners.
briefs.action_required: If you own a building in Burnaby, consider signing up for free energy benchmarking at buildingbenchmarkbc.ca.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Building Benchmark BC Year 7 participation approval

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Council to vote on granting FortisBC a right-of-way for natural gas work at Burnaby Mountain
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on giving FortisBC Energy Inc. a Statutory Right-of-Way — a legal permission to access and use a strip of city-owned land — over parts of two properties near Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area. This would allow FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas infrastructure needed to service the Mintara restaurant and protect an existing gas line that has been running without proper legal access since the 1960s.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business operates near Burnaby Mountain or relies on FortisBC gas service in that area.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — FortisBC Burnaby Mountain right-of-way approval

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Natural Resources, Food Service

briefs.title: Council to vote on: FortisBC natural gas right-of-way on Burnaby Mountain City land
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on granting FortisBC Energy Inc. a statutory right-of-way — a legal permission to use a strip of City-owned land — over portions of 7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way. This would allow FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas infrastructure needed to service the Mintara restaurant and other facilities in the Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area. The City would receive no payment for this land access.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 14, 2026 Council meeting if your business operates near Burnaby Mountain or relies on FortisBC gas service in that area.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — FortisBC Burnaby Mountain right-of-way approval

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Natural Resources, Food Service

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.35M contract for modular four-plex housing pilot in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,354,463 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular four-plex (a factory-built, four-unit residential building) at 8315 14th Avenue as a pilot project. The goal is to test whether this style of construction is a cost-effective and low-carbon way to add small-scale rental housing in lower-density neighbourhoods.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — this pilot could shape future modular housing contracts in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — modular four-plex contract award decision

briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.4M contract for Jerry Rogers Creek culvert replacement in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $2,404,076 contract to HANNA Infrastructure Ltd. to replace an undersized culvert at Jerry Rogers Creek with a larger box culvert built to handle major flood events. The project also includes underground utility work. Funding comes from federal gas tax reserves and a drainage development charge reserve fund.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business operates near Jerry Rogers Creek or Marine Drive.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Jerry Rogers Creek culvert contract award

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.36M contract for Central Valley Greenway culvert and paving work
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,360,391 contract to Drake Excavating (2016) Ltd. to replace 16 culverts (underground drainage pipes) along the Central Valley Greenway trail and complete final paving between Sperling Avenue and Douglas Road. The project is already budgeted in the City's 2026–2030 Financial Plan, with funding split between City reserves and TransLink.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business operates near the Central Valley Greenway trail.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Central Valley Greenway culvert contract approval

briefs.title: Council to vote on $450,000 contract extension with Rogers for city cellular services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving a $450,000 contract change (an increase to an existing agreement) with Rogers Communications Canada Inc. to keep the city's cellular voice and data services running while the city switches to a new provider. This is a short-term, bridging arrangement — not a long-term deal — and a new contract with a different provider is expected to come before Council at a future meeting.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $450,000 contract extension for Rogers cellular services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change that would extend the city's existing cellular voice and data services agreement with Rogers Communications Canada Inc. by up to nine months, at an estimated cost of $450,000 including taxes. This short-term extension would keep city phones and data plans running while Burnaby finishes switching to a new service provider found through a public bidding process.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 14, 2026 — this is an internal city contract with no direct impact on most businesses.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Rogers cellular contract change approval

briefs.title: Council to vote on $579,600 contract extension for Burnaby city building HVAC services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on extending its heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) maintenance and repair contract with Just Mechanical Ltd. for an estimated $579,600 including taxes. This short-term extension would keep city buildings running smoothly while the City completes a new competitive bidding process to find a long-term service provider.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — this affects HVAC service contracts with the City of Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — HVAC contract extension approval meeting

briefs.title: Council to vote on $579,600 contract change for City building HVAC maintenance
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a contract change worth $579,600 (including taxes) with Just Mechanical Ltd. to keep heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems running in City-owned buildings. This is a short-term extension to avoid any gap in service while the City runs a new competitive bidding process to find a long-term provider.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business supplies HVAC or facilities services to the City.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — HVAC contract change vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on $394,942 renewal for Burnaby's phone support software licence
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a three-year software licence with Long View Systems Corporation for approximately $394,942 (taxes included). The licence covers Cisco WebEx Contact Center — the system that runs the City's phone-based customer service and IT support lines — for the period May 5, 2026 to May 4, 2029.
briefs.action_required: No direct action needed — this is an internal City IT contract with no impact on most businesses.

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Burnaby's $394,942 Cisco WebEx Contact Center license renewal

The contract would keep the City's phone-based customer service system running and support IT staff phone support from May 5, 2026 to May 4, 2029.
briefs.action_required: No direct action needed — this is an internal City IT contract with no impact on local businesses.

briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with A.W. Fireguard Supplies (1991) Ltd. to inspect, maintain, and repair fire protection and emergency lighting systems at City-owned buildings. The proposed three-year renewal would run from July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2029, at an estimated cost of $1,666,061 including taxes — with the same pricing as the current contract.
briefs.action_required: If you supply fire protection services, watch this contract — it signals Burnaby's procurement activity in this space.

briefs.title: Council to vote on external financial review and 20-storey Beresford Street rezoning
briefs.summary: At its April 14, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council is set to vote on two key committee items: hiring an outside reviewer to check the City's financial practices and reduce the risk of future tax increases, and deciding whether to advance a proposed 20-storey apartment building at 5035 Beresford Street to its first formal readings.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 14 Council meeting — the financial review and Beresford rezoning votes could affect local taxes and development.

briefs.title: Council to vote on hiring external reviewer to assess Burnaby's financial practices
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on hiring an outside expert to review how the City manages its finances, plans spending, and sets priorities for major projects. The goal is to protect the City's financial health and reduce the risk of significant tax hikes or higher service costs for businesses and residents.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — it could signal future tax or service cost changes.

key_dates.label: Burnaby external financial review — Council vote takes place

briefs.industries: Finance & Insurance, Professional Services, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Council to vote on hiring an external reviewer to assess Burnaby's financial practices
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on hiring an outside financial expert to review how the City plans its budget, manages day-to-day spending, and prioritizes major investments. The review is being proposed because density bonus revenues — fees paid by developers to build bigger — have dropped sharply, and the cost of goods and services the City buys has risen significantly, making it harder to keep tax increases modest.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it could signal future changes to business taxes or city service costs.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — external financial review approval

briefs.industries: Finance & Insurance, Construction & Development, Professional Services

briefs.title: Proposed: 20-Storey Rental Apartment at 5035 Beresford Street Up for Council Approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to move forward with a rezoning application that would allow a 20-storey rental apartment building with 215 units at 5035 Beresford Street in the Royal Oak area. The site currently holds a printing shop and would need to be rezoned — meaning the rules about what can be built there would change — before construction could begin.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the upcoming Council meeting for the rezoning bylaw's First and Second Reading date.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ #25-11 rezoning bylaw First and Second Reading — Council meeting

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Proposed: 20-Storey Rental Apartment Building at 5035 Beresford Street, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on advancing a rezoning application that would allow a developer to replace an existing printing shop at 5035 Beresford Street with a 20-storey rental apartment building containing 215 units. The proposed bylaw would change the land's zoning — the rules about what can be built there — from industrial use to a residential apartment district under the Royal Oak Community Plan.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council meeting agendas for the First and Second Reading vote date on this rezoning bylaw.

key_dates.label: REZ #25-11 Burnaby rezoning bylaw — First and Second Reading council vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider North Road BIA Renewal and Economic Growth Task Force Update
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on several items at its April 14, 2026 meeting, including a proposed renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where local businesses pool funds for area improvements — and an update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience. Council will also consider a report that a developer has chosen to forfeit (give up) security funds rather than build a public amenity pier at a Burnaby business park.
briefs.action_required: If your business is in the North Road BIA, watch the April 14 Council vote — the BIA renewal outcome directly affects your area levy.

key_dates.label: Burnaby City Council meeting — North Road BIA renewal vote and consent agenda items

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Progress Update on Burnaby's Economic Growth Task Force
briefs.summary: Council will consider an update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience, a group of business and community leaders working to build a stronger local economy. The Task Force has held five workshops so far and is now gathering feedback from businesses through surveys and interviews, with final recommendations expected to be presented to Council on June 23, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Task Force's final recommendations to Council on June 23, 2026 — they may affect local business support programs.

key_dates.label: Mayor's Task Force final recommendations presented to Burnaby Council
Mayor's Task Force workshop sessions resume — Burnaby Economic Growth Task Force

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Developer Forfeits $1.36M Instead of Building Public Pier
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report on Oxford Properties' decision to give up $1,363,500 in security — money held as a guarantee — rather than build a planned public pier on the Fraser River at Riverbend Business Park. The developer could not get the required provincial land approval to build on the river, so under a legal agreement already in place, the City will keep the funds and may use them for a future public amenity along the Fraser Foreshore or elsewhere in Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Watch for how Council decides to spend the $1.36M — it could fund a public amenity near your business.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Information Reports — April 14, 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review a set of information reports at its April 14, 2026 meeting. No body text or details were provided for this agenda item, so the specific topics and any potential impacts on local businesses are not yet known.
briefs.action_required: Check the full April 14, 2026 Burnaby Council agenda for report details that may affect your business.

briefs.title: Council to vote on North Road BIA renewal — sufficient objections received
briefs.summary: At its April 14, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council is set to vote on the final adoption of Bylaw #14797, which would renew the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a defined zone where businesses pay a levy to fund local improvements — for a 5-year period. However, the agenda notes that enough property owners have filed formal petitions against the renewal to meet the legal threshold, which could block the bylaw from passing.
briefs.action_required: If you are a North Road BIA member, watch the April 14 council vote — the outcome directly affects your BIA levy.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA renewal bylaw — council final adoption vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on dissolving Burnaby North Road BIA renewal due to sufficient petitions against
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on the final adoption of Bylaw #14797, which would renew the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a defined zone where businesses collectively fund local improvements — for another 5 years. However, the agenda notes that enough business owners have signed petitions against the renewal to potentially block it, meaning Council will consider whether to proceed or halt the bylaw.
briefs.action_required: If you operate in the North Road BIA zone, attend the April 14 Council meeting to follow the vote outcome.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA renewal bylaw — Council final adoption vote

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area Bylaw — Council to vote on final adoption
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would officially create the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a defined zone where businesses pool funds for local improvements like street beautification, graffiti removal, and business promotion. If passed, property owners in the zone classified as light industry or business properties would pay a special local tax to fund up to $1.75 million in grants to the BIA association over five years (April 2026–March 2031). Importantly, enough property owners have already filed formal objections to trigger a reconsideration process, so the outcome is not certain.
briefs.action_required: If your property is in the Burnaby North Road BIA zone, contact Burnaby City Hall now to confirm your status and tax impact.

briefs.title: Council to Announce 2026 Bursary Awards for Eight Burnaby Secondary Students
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to approve bursary awards (one-time financial grants) for eight secondary school students across Burnaby schools, including École Alpha, Burnaby Central, Burnaby Mountain, and others. Council will also authorize staff to notify the recipients and their school principals, with the announcement planned for the April 14, 2026 Open Council meeting.
